hypothesis: Finite-depth phase universality can be sharpened into a monotone witness-complexity criterion whose boundedness is exactly finite support.
interpretation: Minimum normalized phase witness lengths are monotone; boundedness is equivalent to one finite word killing the complete future boundary. Counting gives an almost-sure linear lower bound log(2)/log(3).
limitations: does not prove witness complexity diverges for the actual zero-initialized fringe schedule
does not prove the alternating inverse lift has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: Monotonicity, the boundedness criterion, counting theorem, and almost-sure lower bound are all-depth proofs in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_witness_complexity.md. Schreier-graph values are bounded exact computations.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions.almost_sure_linear_bound: for Bernoulli-almost every schedule and each phase a, liminf kappa_a(q,L)/L >= log(2)/log(3)
exact_conclusions.boundedness_criterion: a finite normalized phase-a word kills the full infinite boundary iff sup_L kappa_a(q,L) is finite
exact_conclusions.counting_bound: at depth L, at most (3^N-1)/2 schedule prefixes have phase-a witness complexity at most N
exact_conclusions.remaining_target: prove both phase complexities diverge on the exact zero-initialized fringe schedule

result_summary.scope_warning: the depth campaign is finite and does not prove divergence for the actual schedule
